title: Podman attempts to disable LRO on a device that doesn't support it (x86_64)
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**
Podman attempts to disable LRO on a device that doesn't support it (x86_64)
<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Run https://github.com/CKI-project/tests-beaker/blob/master/container/podman/runtest.sh

2.

3.

**Describe the results you received:**
 WARNING: CPU: 1 PID: 4593 at net/core/dev.c:1595 dev_disable_lro+0xb2/0xf0  messages seen in the console log and related call trace

console log:
https://cki-artifacts.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/datawarehouse/2020/04/13/528025/x86_64_7_console.log

podman test log: https://cki-artifacts.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/index.html?prefix=datawarehouse/2020/04/13/528025/Podman_system_integration_test___as_root/
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
No warning, test is skipped if unsupported

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 1.8.2
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

```

kvm guest

comments.body:
Where is podman trying to turn LRO off?   Is podman even capable of doing such a thing?

If not podman what is (and where?)  Was cni configuration trying to turn LRO off (e.g. via tuning plugin)?  I don't see the cni .conflist so I can't check for myself.

@rasibley 

Is `ens3` a host device? Is there a host-device plugin at play here, maybe?

Providing the `podman run` line for the container that caused this, and any CNI config files present in `/etc/cni/net.d/` would help diagnose.

Do you have a reproducer? Can you make this happen consistently?

Looking at the linked script, I can't see a single `podman run` command. @mccv1r0 This does not sound like something that would ever happen on CNI init, right? We'd need some sort of explicit call to the plugins to set up a pod to make changes to the host?
Ah, I see that this runs the system tests. Where are those tests located? Where can I get a copy of them?

I also can't see any correlation (timestamps, etc) between the different logs provided. As such, it's extremely difficult to tell which of the tests is causing this failure. I don't know how helpful we can be without knowing that.

It's triggers during "podman images - history output"
```

It is consistently reproducible with "podman images - history output" test on 5.6.4-300.fc31.x86_64.

ens3 is only guest NIC other than loopback:
```
# ethtool -i ens3
driver: virtio_net
version: 1.0.0
firmware-version:
expansion-rom-version:
bus-info: 0000:00:03.0
supports-statistics: yes
supports-test: no
supports-eeprom-access: no
supports-register-dump: no
supports-priv-flags: no
```

```
# ll /etc/cni/net.d/
total 4
-rw-r--r--. 1 root root 612 Mar 20 12:54 87-podman-bridge.conflist
# cat /etc/cni/net.d/87-podman-bridge.conflist
{
  "cniVersion": "0.4.0",
  "name": "podman",
  "plugins": [
    {
      "type": "bridge",
      "bridge": "cni-podman0",
      "isGateway": true,
      "ipMasq": true,
      "ipam": {
        "type": "host-local",
        "routes": [{ "dst": "0.0.0.0/0" }],
        "ranges": [
          [
            {
              "subnet": "10.88.0.0/16",
              "gateway": "10.88.0.1"
            }
          ]
        ]
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "portmap",
      "capabilities": {
        "portMappings": true
      }
    },
    {
      "type": "firewall"
    },
    {
      "type": "tuning"
    }
  ]
}
```

Per systemtap parent of 'bridge' is podman:
```
WARNING: CPU: 0 PID: 947 at net/core/dev.c:1595 dev_disable_lro+0xb2/0xf0

PPID    PID           COMM ARGS
905    947         bridge /usr/libexec/cni/bridge
904    905        timeout timeout --foreground -v --kill=10 60 podman run --name my-container docker.io/library/alpine:latest true
```

But actual trigger appears to be:
```
(gdb) f 13
#13 0x0000560a74a54cd7 in github.com/containernetworking/plugins/pkg/ip.EnableIP4Forward ()
    at /usr/src/debug/containernetworking-plugins-0.8.5-1.fc31.x86_64/gopath/src/github.com/containernetworking/plugins/pkg/ip/ipforward_linux.go:25
25              return echo1("/proc/sys/net/ipv4/ip_forward")

```

Assuming this is our system tests in master, which now seems likely: likely culprit is https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/31d14457a5636df62c755480ee10f1946a5bc8b0/test/system/010-images.bats#L50

@mccv1r0 From this, it looks like it is CNI, but it also reproduces trivially by echoing into /proc manually; does this smell like a potential issue outside Podman/CNI (kernel, maybe?) to you?

Yes.  Looks like a kernel bug.

CNI/Podman  just use the kernel bridge.  CNI/podman don't touch forwarding or LRO settings.

Thanks Jan for providing more debug details, it's easily reproducible and I'm seeing it on older kernels as well (kernel-5.3.11-100.fc29.x86_64), not sure how long this has been around.  If this is a kernel bug, feel free to close and I can report elsewhere.
Open a bugzilla on the kernel and link to this issue.  If they kernel teams throw it back at us, we can reopen this issue.
